name: Chocolate Sauce
recipeIngredient: 2 ounces semisweet baking chocolate,  see note
1 teaspoon vanilla extract or some other flavoring
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt the baking chocolate in the top of a double boiler.
2. Stir in the butter.
3. Add the boiling water and mix well.
4. Add the sugar and salt, stirring occasionally until dissolved.
5. Cook for about 15 minutes, stirring only a few times.
6. Remove from heat and stir in the vanilla extract or desired flavoring.
7. Serve immediately or cool and store in a covered container in the refrigerator. The sauce will keep up to 3 months and is best if reheated slightly.
YIELD: about 1 cup of sauce
Some flavor variations to use instead of vanilla extract:
- 1 tsp mint extract
- 2 Tbsp chopped nuts
- 1 tsp almond extract
- 1 tsp instant coffee granules
- 1 tsp orange extract
- 1 tsp maraschino cherry syrup and 2 Tbsp chopped cherries
- 1 tbsp apricot preserves
